message: you have open user requests this session but no sign of maintaining the visible ledger (no Edit/Write/NotebookEdit to .brainer/ledger/<sid>.md and no TaskCreate/TaskUpdate) in the recent window — materialize or refresh .brainer/ledger/<sid>.md (the file is authoritative; native tasks mirror it) so an item the user raised is not silently dropped. Decompose each user message into ATOMIC rows. NEVER DELETE A ROW — only re-status (open/doing/done/deferred); a row that vanishes between edits is the same silent-drop failure this probe exists to catch.
_note: SKILL.md's 'never delete a row — only re-status' rule has no dedicated drift probe: none of hook.py's implemented DETECTORS kinds can diff ledger content ACROSS turns to notice a row disappearing (forbidden_regex/claim_without_evidence etc. only see current-window text, not a structural diff of a markdown table over time). Rather than invent an unimplemented kind (the canary hook would silently skip it — an alive-looking-dead gate), the rule is folded into this probe's message so at least the maintenance-absence case still surfaces it. Honest degradation, not full mechanical coverage.

message: this done-status line names something the USER will do (or a deferred/third-person action) — that is a self-close on an interpretation you never got confirmed, not a verified done. Live incident: 'done — byte-identical copy (scaffold; Saar sorts out art)' shipped a scaffold as done while quietly assuming the user would separately do the art pass. Covers done/complete/finished/closed claims, no-parens phrasing, one sentence-boundary crossing, and any third-person verb ('handles', 'sorts') or 'will'/'later'/'afterwards' deferral — but not when a strong evidence token (verified/N-of-N/render/test/manifest/matched) appears BETWEEN the done-word and the assumption phrase (position-bound: trailing evidence AFTER the assumption clause does not suppress). A future/deferral marker ('tomorrow', 'next') anywhere in the same done-line OVERRIDES the evidence-token suppression outright — evidence of past work never excuses a still-deferred user action, so 'done — tests pass; Saar sorts out art tomorrow' fires even though 'tests' is a strong evidence token. Present-confirmation verbs (agrees, confirms, approves, accepts, acknowledges, signs off) are excluded from the third-person-verb generalization — those describe a completed confirmation, not a deferred/future user action, so 'user agrees this is complete' and 'Saar confirms receipt' stay silent. _note: threat model is unintentional drift; a deliberately fabricated evidence token is verify-before-completion's domain, not this regex. Re-status as doing/deferred until the assumption is confirmed, or state the assumption OUTSIDE the done-claim and ask before closing.
_note: ADVISORY heuristic over unbounded natural language — observed-incident phrasings + common variants are covered and regression-tested (3 adversarial rounds); paraphrase false-negatives and topic-collision false-positives remain possible by design and are accepted (LEARNING_CONTRACT §3: heuristic banked with scope of validity). Residual (accepted, not fixed here): 'will'/'later'/'afterwards' are NOT added to the deferral-override lookahead alongside 'tomorrow'/'next' — a locked regression case ('done — verified 31/31 via manifest (literal phrase user will was covered by the negative test)') requires a quoted/attributive mention of 'will' to stay silent when evidence is present, and a bare-word override for 'will'/'later'/'afterwards' would collide with that quoted-mention case (both are indistinguishable to a regex). A genuine deferred close using 'later'/'afterwards'/'will' phrasing WITH intervening evidence text will therefore still be silenced — an accepted false-negative narrower than the 'tomorrow'/'next' fix.
